subject: [PATCH v5 05/21] mm/swap: add a new function to check if a swap entry is in swap cached.

2026-03-20.patch_summary: This patch adds a new function called `is_swap_cached` to the Linux kernel's memory management (mm) module. The function checks if a given swap entry is cached in the system's swap cache. This is done by directly accessing the swap map of the corresponding swap information structure, which minimizes overhead and ensures accurate results. The patch also updates several other functions to use this new function instead of directly checking the swap map. The motivation behind this change is to abstract away the check for swap cached state, as it will soon be managed at the virtual swap layer.
